meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

source_tables.name: 14-10-0416-01 Labour force characteristics by occupation, annual

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services
family.definition: This major group comprises retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 2. Occupations classified in TEER 2, as those included in this group with a 2 as the second digit of their code, typically require completion of a post-secondary education program of two to three years at community college, institute of technology or CÉGEP; or completion of an apprenticeship training program of two to five years; or supervisory or significant safety responsibilities; or the accumulation of several years of experience in a related occupation found in TEER 3, when applicable (TEER 3 includes occupations with a 3 as a second digit in their code).
family.major_group_title: Retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

units.title: Retail sales supervisors
units.major_group_title: Retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services
units.definition: Retail sales supervisors supervise and coordinate the activities of workers in the following unit groups: Retail salespersons and visual merchandisers (64100), Cashiers (65100), Store shelf stockers, clerks and order fillers (65102) and Other sales related occupations (65109). They are employed by stores and other retail businesses, wholesale businesses that sell on a retail basis to the public, rental service establishments and businesses involved in door-to-door soliciting and telemarketing.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Sales and service supervisors (62, 63).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Retail sales supervisors supervise and coordinate the activities of workers in the following unit groups: Retail salespersons and visual merchandisers (64.100), Cashiers (65.100), Store shelf stockers, clerks and order fillers (65.102) and Other sales related occupations (65.109).
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Previous retail sales experience as a retail salesperson or sales clerk, cashier, telemarketer, door-to-door salesperson or rental agent is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to management positions in retail trade is possible with additional training or experience.
workplaces_employers: Businesses involved in door-to-door soliciting and telemarketing
Wholesale businesses that sell on a retail basis

core_competencies.statement: to handle customer complaints or requests for refunds to find balanced solutions that keep customers satisfied while protecting the store’s interests
to instruct new hires on store policies, procedures, product knowledge and customer service techniques to ensure they perform their duties correctly
to clearly assign tasks or give directions to employees to avoid confusion or mistakes
to promptly recognize product shortages, pricing errors or equipment malfunctions to avoid disruptions and sustain productivity
to motivate employees, foster a positive work environment and create a welcoming environment for customers
to understand sales techniques, promotional strategies and customer behaviour to guide teams to meet sales targets and optimize product placements

units.title: Food service supervisors
units.major_group_title: Retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services
units.definition: Food service supervisors supervise, direct and coordinate the activities of workers who prepare, portion and serve food. They are employed by hospitals and other health care establishments and by cafeterias, catering companies and other food service establishments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Sales and service supervisors (62, 63).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Food service supervisors supervise, direct and coordinate the activities of workers who prepare, portion and serve food.
employment_requirements: Completion of a community college program in food service administration, hotel and restaurant management or related discipline orseveral years of experience in food preparation or service are required.
Completion of secondary school is usually required.

example_titles.job_title_text: railway dining car food service supervisor

core_competencies.statement: to coordinate the timing of food preparation, cooking and serving so orders are completed promptly and efficiently, especially during peak hours
to oversee food preparations by staff to ensure items are cooked to safe temperatures and sanitation procedures are followed
to communicate instructions to workers to avoid misunderstandings or errors in food preparation, service or safety violations
to quickly recognize issues that can affect the efficiency and quality of food service operations
to tolerate and manage stress to remain calm, make quick decisions and maintain control over operations, ensuring service runs smoothly
to understand principles in client services to address clients concerns, handle complaints and train employees for customer interactions

units.title: Executive housekeepers
units.major_group_title: Retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services
units.definition: Executive housekeepers direct and control the operations of housekeeping departments within hotels, hospitals and other establishments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Sales and service supervisors (62, 63).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Executive housekeepers direct and control the operations of housekeeping departments within hotels, hospitals and other establishments.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
A university degree or college diploma in hospital management, hotel management or business administration is usually required.
Extensive experience as a cleaning supervisor may substitute for formal education requirements.

core_competencies.statement: to assess the performance of housekeeping staff, including evaluating efficiency, adherence to protocols and overall effectiveness
to oversee a list of tasks and responsibilities to ensure all rooms and common areas are cleaned to high standards within the expected period
to perform quick and repetitive tasks, such as folding linens, making beds, handling cleaning tools and conducting inspections
to safely lift and move cleaning supplies and occasionally move furniture or equipment
to coordinate, motivate and maintain high standards of cleanliness with housekeeping staff
to understand client service principles to address guests' needs and preferences, resolve complaints and train other housekeepers for client interactions

units.title: Accommodation, travel, tourism and related services supervisors
units.major_group_title: Retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services
units.definition: Accommodation, travel, tourism and related services supervisors supervise and coordinate the activities of hotel accommodation service clerks, casino workers, reservation clerks and other travel and accommodations workers not elsewhere classified. They are employed by service establishments throughout the public and private sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Accommodation, travel, tourism and related services supervisors
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Accommodation, travel, tourism and related services supervisors
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Accommodation, travel, tourism and related services supervisors supervise and coordinate the activities of hotel accommodation service clerks, casino workers, reservation clerks and other travel and accommodations workers.
employment_requirements: Experience in the occupation supervised is usually required.
Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Casino gaming licences are usually required for all casino employees.
Completion of college courses related to the area supervised may be required.
additional_information: Progression to managerial positions is possible with additional training or experience.
workplaces_employers: Service establishments throughout the public and private sectors

example_titles.job_title_text: airline sales and service agents supervisor

core_competencies.statement: to ensure necessary supplies are well stocked, such as linens, cleaning supplies and guest amenities
to establish work schedules and co-ordinate activities with other work units and departments
to clearly provide instructions, feedback and information to staff and guests
to identify and resolve issues with the overall quality of service and operational effectiveness in the hospitality and travel sector
to resolve work-related problems, remain calm and maintain control over operations during peak times to provide good service
to understand hospitality principles which allows to create a welcoming experience for guests, to anticipate and address effectively guests' needs and preferences

units.title: Customer and information services supervisors
units.major_group_title: Retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services
units.definition: Customer and information services supervisors supervise and coordinate the activities of workers in the following unit groups: Customer service representatives - financial institutions (64400) and Other customer and information services representatives (64409). They are employed by banks, trust companies, credit unions and similar financial institutions, retail establishments, contact centres, insurance, telephone and utility companies and other establishments throughout the public and private sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. oasis_profiles.profile_title: Customer service representatives supervisors - financial services
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Customer service representatives supervisors - financial services supervise and co-ordinate the activities of workers in the following group: Customer service representatives - financial institutions (64.400).
main_duties: Customer service representatives supervisors - financial services

Supervise, coordinate, assign and review the work of clerks engaged in the following duties: providing information and customer service; and serving customers in banks, trust companies and other financial institutions
Examine and verify accuracy of work and authorize routine deposits and withdrawals
Establish work schedules and procedures
Coordinate activities with other work units or departments
Resolve work-related problems and prepare and submit progress and other reports
Identify training needs and train workers in job duties and company policies
Ensure smooth operation of computer systems and equipment and arrange for maintenance and repair work
May perform the same duties as workers supervised.
employment_requirements: Experience in the occupation supervised is required.
Completion of college or industry courses related to financial or customer services may be required.
workplaces_employers: Establishments throughout the private and public sectors

example_titles.job_title_text: customer service representatives supervisor - financial services

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Customer service representatives supervisors (except financial services)
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Customer service representatives supervisors (except financial services) supervise and co-ordinate the activities of workers in the following group: Other customer and information services representatives (64409).
main_duties: Supervise, coordinate, assign, and review the work of clerks and agents engaged in conducting surveys and interviews, collecting and compiling statistics, providing information and customer service, and writing correspondence including emails and preparing materials
Establish work schedules and procedures
Coordinate activities with other units and departments
Prepare and submit progress reports and other documentation
Identify training needs and train workers in job duties and company policies
Ensure the smooth operation of computer systems and equipment and arrange for maintenance and repair work
May perform the same duties as workers supervised.
employment_requirements: Experience in the occupation supervised is required.
Completion of college or industry courses related to financial or customer services may be required.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

units.title: Cleaning supervisors
units.major_group_title: Retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services
units.definition: Cleaning supervisors supervise and coordinate the activities of workers in the following unit groups: Light duty cleaners (65310), Specialized cleaners (65311), and Janitors, caretakers and building superintendents (73201). They are employed by hospitals and other health care institutions, hotels, motels, schools and other educational institutions, commercial and industrial establishments, home and office cleaning establishments and by various specialized cleaning companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Cleaning supervisors supervise and coordinate the activities of workers in the following unit groups: Light duty cleaners (65.310), Specialized cleaners (65.311), and Janitors, caretakers and building superintendents (73.201).
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Previous experience in a particular area of cleaning is usually required.
Previous supervisory experience may be required.
workplaces_employers: Hospitals and other health care institutions

core_competencies.statement: to allocate time effectively to various tasks and responsibilities, including overseeing staff, managing schedules and ensuring cleaning tasks are completed on time
to assess the performance of individual team members and to identify areas where additional training or support might be needed
to supervise and co-ordinate the work of light duty, industrial and specialized cleaners and janitors
to perform physical tasks, such as lifting cleaning equipment, moving supplies, or demonstrating physically demanding cleaning techniques
to quickly reassign cleaning tasks and adjust priorities to meet evolving requirements
to prepare work schedules for cleaners and coordinate activities with those of other departments
to understand client service principles to ensure client satisfaction, increase repeat business or referrals and any complaints regarding cleaning services are handled professionally

units.title: Other services supervisors
units.major_group_title: Retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services
units.definition: Other services supervisors supervise and coordinate the activities of dry cleaning, laundry, ironing, pressing and finishing workers, theatre ushers and attendants, spa, sports and recreation club workers, commissionaires, security guards and other service workers. They are employed by service establishments throughout the public and private sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Other services supervisors supervise and coordinate the activities of dry cleaning, laundry, ironing, pressing and finishing workers, theatre ushers and attendants, spa, sports and recreation club workers, commissionaires, security guards and other service workers.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of college courses related to the area supervised may be required.
Experience in the occupation supervised is usually required.
Certification or licensing may be required for some occupations in this group (i.e., supervisors of commissionaires or security guards).
additional_information: Progression to managerial positions is possible with additional training or experience.
workplaces_employers: Service establishments throughout the public and private sectors

example_titles.job_title_text: steam press operators foreman/woman - laundry and dry cleaning

core_competencies.statement: to hire and train employees on policies and duties to meet service demand and ensure smooth operations
to organize workflows, set priorities and ensure operations run without delays
to address multiple issues at once, such as resolving conflicts, handling customer complaints and managing staff performance
to adjust their management approach based on the specific environment and the nature of the service being provided
to identify issues with service operation or customer satisfaction to prevent them from escalating into major disruptions
to closely review requisition requests and work reports for errors or to determine requirements and progress
to understand best practices for customer service to create positive experiences and maintain customer satisfaction

units.title: Technical sales specialists - wholesale trade
units.major_group_title: Retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services
units.minor_group_title: Technical sales specialists in wholesale trade and retail and wholesale buyers
units.definition: Technical sales specialists in wholesale trade, sell a range of technical goods and services, such as scientific, agricultural and industrial products, electricity, telecommunications services and computer services, to governments and to commercial and industrial establishments in domestic and international localities. They are employed by establishments that produce or provide technical goods and services, such as pharmaceutical companies, industrial equipment manufacturing companies, grain elevators, computer services firms, engineering firms and hydroelectric companies, or they may be self-employed technical sales specialists/agents who contract their services to other companies. Technical sales specialists in wholesale trade who are supervisors are included in this unit group.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Sales and service supervisors (62, 63).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Technical sales specialists, wholesale trade, sell a range of technical goods and services, such as scientific, agricultural and industrial products, electricity, telecommunications services and computer services, to governments and to commercial and industrial establishments in domestic and international localities. Technical sales specialists in wholesale trade who are supervisors are included in this unit group.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in a program related to the product or service is usually required.
Experience in sales or in a technical occupation related to the product or service may be required.

Fluency in a specific foreign language, and/or foreign work or travel experience may be required for technical sales specialists seeking employment with companies that import or export technical goods or services.
Technical sales supervisors require experience as a technical sales specialist.
A driver's licence and a good driving record may be required.
additional_information: Extensive company training programs for technical sales specialists may be provided by employers.
Progression to sales management positions is possible with additional training or experience.
Technical sales specialists usually specialize in a particular line of goods or services.

example_titles.job_title_text: agricultural equipment and supplies technical salesperson
commercial and industrial equipment and supplies sales representative - technical wholesale
electronic data-processing (EDP) hardware and services sales representative
lighting, heating and power sales representative
plumbing and heating services sales representative
light, heat and power sales engineer
railway equipment and supplies technical salesperson
lighting, heating and power sales specialist
railway equipment and supplies technical sales specialist

core_competencies.statement: to ensure products are stocked to meet client demands while preventing stock shortages or overstock situations
to negotiate prices, delivery schedules and product specifications to meet both the client’s needs and the company’s objectives
to explain products clearly and accurately to clients, ensuring they understand the technical aspects and benefits
to listen to complex information conveyed by clients or during presentations
to analyze client requirements carefully and propose solutions that precisely match their needs to enhance customer satisfaction
to understand clients' needs and resources to recommend the appropriate goods and services

units.title: Retail and wholesale buyers
units.major_group_title: Retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services
units.minor_group_title: Technical sales specialists in wholesale trade and retail and wholesale buyers
units.definition: Retail and wholesale buyers buy merchandise for resale by retail or wholesale establishments and are usually responsible for the merchandising operations of retail or wholesale establishments. Retail and wholesale buyers who are supervisors and those who are assistants are included in this unit group.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Sales and service supervisors (62, 63).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Retail and wholesale buyers buy merchandise for resale by retail or wholesale establishments and are usually responsible for the merchandising operations of retail or wholesale establishments. Retail and wholesale buyers who are supervisors and those who are assistants are included in this unit group.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in business, marketing or a related program is usually required.
Completion of secondary school is required.
Previous work experience in retail or wholesale establishments is usually required.
Supervisors and senior buyers require experience in the occupation supervised or in a particular merchandise line.
additional_information: Progression to management positions in retail trade, sales, marketing or advertising is possible with additional training or experience.
Retail and wholesale buyers may specialize through experience or product-related training courses.
Retail and wholesale buyers may specialize in a particular merchandise line.

core_competencies.statement: to negotiate contracts and coordinate with suppliers to ensure the right products are purchased at the best prices and shipped at the correct times
to acquire the essential goods while optimizing costs to ensure sustained profitability
to clearly discuss terms of agreements to avoid misunderstandings and ensure agreements are mutually understood
to review past sales data and identify patterns to determine which products are likely to sell well in the future
to collaborate with merchandising teams to align product selection with marketing strategies, store layouts and promotions
to understand retail and wholesale budgets to calculate the cost of goods and monitoring expenses

units.title: Chefs
units.major_group_title: Retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services
units.definition: Chefs plan and direct food preparation and cooking activities and who prepare and cook meals and specialty foods. They are employed in restaurants, hotels, hospitals and other health care institutions, central food commissaries, clubs and similar establishments, and on ships.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Sales and service supervisors (62, 63).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Head chefs plan and direct food preparation and cooking activities and prepare and cook meals and specialty foods.
main_duties: Plan and direct food preparation and cooking activities of several restaurants in an establishment, restaurant chains, hospitals or other establishments with food services
Consult with clients regarding weddings, banquets and specialty functions
Plan menus and ensure food meets quality standards
Estimate food requirements and may estimate food and labour costs
Supervise activities of sous-chefs, specialist chefs, chefs and cooks
Arrange for equipment purchases and repairs
Recruit and hire staff
May prepare and cook food on a regular basis, or for special guests or functions.
employment_requirements: Cook's trade certification or equivalent credentials, training and experience are required.

The certifications of Certified Working Chef (CWC) and Certified Chef de Cuisine (CCC), administered by the Canadian Culinary Institute (CCI) of the Canadian Culinary Federation (CCF) are available to qualified chefs.
Red Seal endorsement for cooks is also available to qualified chefs upon successful completion of the interprovincial Red Seal examination.

Executive chefs usually require management training and several years of experience in commercial food preparation, including two years in a supervisory capacity andexperience as a sous-chef, specialist chef or chef.
Completion of secondary school is usually required.
additional_information: The Red Seal endorsement allows for interprovincial mobility.
Executive chefs may progress to managerial positions in food preparation establishments.
There is some mobility among the various types of chefs in this unit group.
workplaces_employers: Hospitals and other health care institutions

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Sous-chefs plan and direct food preparation and cooking activities. They may prepare and cook meals and specialty foods.
main_duties: Supervise activities of specialist chefs, chefs, cooks and other kitchen workers
Demonstrate new cooking techniques and new equipment to cooking staff
May plan menus and requisition food and kitchen supplies
May prepare and cook meals or specialty foods.
Instruct cooks in preparation, cooking, garnishing and presentation of food
employment_requirements: Cook's trade certification or equivalent credentials, training and experience are required.
Red Seal endorsement for cooks is also available to qualified chefs upon successful completion of the interprovincial Red Seal examination.
Sous-chefs, specialist chefs and chefs usually require several years of experience in commercial food preparation.
Completion of secondary school is usually required.
additional_information: The Red Seal endorsement allows for interprovincial mobility.
There is some mobility among the various types of chefs in this unit group.
workplaces_employers: Hospitals and other health care institutions

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Chefs and specialist chefs prepare and cook meals and specialty foods. They also plan and supervise cooking activities.
main_duties: Prepare and cook complete meals or specialty foods, such as pastries, sauces, soups, salads, vegetables and meat, poultry and fish dishes, and create decorative food displays for special events such as banquets
Instruct cooks in preparation, cooking, garnishing and presentation of food
Create new recipes
Supervise cooks and other kitchen staff
May plan menus
May requisition food and kitchen supplies.
employment_requirements: Cook's trade certification or equivalent credentials, training and experience are required.

The certifications of Certified Working Chef (CWC) and Certified Chef de Cuisine (CCC), administered by the Canadian Culinary Institute (CCI) of the Canadian Culinary Federation (CCF) are available to qualified chefs.
Red Seal endorsement for cooks is also available to qualified chefs upon successful completion of the interprovincial Red Seal examination.
Sous-chefs, specialist chefs and chefs usually require several years of experience in commercial food preparation.
Completion of secondary school is usually required.
additional_information: The Red Seal endorsement allows for interprovincial mobility.
There is some mobility among the various types of chefs in this unit group.
workplaces_employers: Hospitals and other health care institutions

units.title: Funeral directors and embalmers
units.major_group_title: Retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services
units.definition: Funeral directors coordinate and arrange all aspects of funeral services. Embalmers prepare the remains of deceased persons for public visitation and burial. Funeral directors and embalmers are employed by funeral homes.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Sales and service supervisors (62, 63).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Funeral directors coordinate and arrange all aspects of funeral services.
main_duties: Consult with the family of the deceased regarding the nature of the funeral service, the disposition of the remains and funeral costs
Transfer, or arrange for the transfer of, the remains from the place of death to the funeral home
Inform survivors of benefits for which they may be eligible
Issue death notices to newspapers
Oversee the preparation of the remains, plan and schedule funeral services, coordinate burials and cremations and complete legal documents
Discuss and negotiate prearranged funerals with clients
Manage funeral home operations including hiring and directing staff, maintaining financial records, preparing accounts and ordering merchandise
Supervise embalmers, funeral home attendants and other funeral home staff
May perform same duties as embalmers.
employment_requirements: Completion of a one- to two-year college program and a twelve- to twenty-month practical apprenticeship program during or following the educational program is usually required for funeral directors.
Funeral directors require licensure in most provinces and territories.
Funeral directors may be required to be licensed embalmers depending on provincial requirements.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Embalmers prepare the remains of deceased persons for public visitation and burial.
main_duties: Preserve, sanitize and prepare human remains for funeral services
Perform cosmetic and restorative work on human remains
Supervise funeral home attendants and other funeral home staff.
employment_requirements: Completion of a two- to three-year college program and a twelve- to twenty-month practical apprenticeship program during or following the educational program orcompletion of a three-year college program is required for embalmers.
Embalmers require licensure in all provinces.

units.title: Jewellers, jewellery and watch repairers and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Retail sales and service supervisors and specialized occupations in sales and services
units.definition: Jewellers and workers in related occupations fabricate, assemble, repair and appraise fine jewellery. Watch repairers and related workers repair, clean, adjust and fabricate parts for clocks and watches. They are employed by jewellery, clock and watch manufacturers and retail stores, by jewellery and watch repair shops or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Sales and service supervisors (62, 63).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Jewellers, jewellery and watch repairers and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Jewellers and workers in related occupations fabricate, assemble, repair and appraise fine jewellery.
main_duties: Fabricate and repair precious and semi-precious metal jewellery, such as rings, brooches, pendants, bracelets and lockets
Examine, cut, shape and polish diamonds using optical instruments, lathes and laps
Examine, cut, shape and polish precious and synthetic gems using optical instruments, cutting disks and laps
Appraise gemstones and diamonds to differentiate between stones, identify rare specimens and to detect peculiarity affecting stone values
Set precious and semi-precious stones in jewellery mountings, according to specifications
May supervise other jewellers.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Experience as a jewellery, clock or watch assembler may be required.
Completion of a college or other program, or apprenticeship training in watch or jewellery repair or gemology is usually required.
additional_information: Mobility is possible among the various jewellery occupations in this unit group.
There is little mobility between the jewellery occupations and the watch repairers in this unit group.
Jewellers may specialize in certain kinds of jewellery, such as gold or silver, or in particular operations, such as fabrication or repair.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Jewellers, jewellery and watch repairers and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Watch repairers and related workers repair, clean, adjust and fabricate parts for clocks and watches.
main_duties: Fabricate or fit parts to make watches and clocks
Examine clocks and watches, disassemble and check for defective and misaligned parts or rust
Replace or repair worn or broken parts
Test, adjust and regulate timepiece movements
Clean all parts using special cleaning and rinsing solutions and ultrasonic or mechanical cleaning machines to remove dirt and dried lubricants
May supervise other watch repairers.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Experience as a jewellery, clock or watch assembler may be required.
Completion of a college or other program, or apprenticeship training in watch or jewellery repair or gemology is usually required.
additional_information: Mobility is possible among the various jewellery occupations in this unit group.
There is little mobility between the jewellery occupations and the watch repairers in this unit group.
